templates.name: Azure by HTTP
templates.description:
This template is designed to monitor Microsoft Azure by HTTP.
It works without any external scripts and uses the script item.
Currently the template supports the discovery of virtual machines (VMs), Cosmos DB for MongoDB, storage accounts, Microsoft SQL, MySQL, and PostgreSQL servers.

Setup:
  1. Create an Azure service principal via the Azure command-line interface (Azure CLI) for your subscription.
    `az ad sp create-for-rbac --name zabbix --role reader --scope /subscriptions/<subscription_id>`
    See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/create-an-azure-service-principal-azure-cli for more details.
  2. Link the template to a host.
  3. Configure the macros: `{$AZURE.APP.ID}`, `{$AZURE.PASSWORD}`, `{$AZURE.TENANT.ID}`, and `{$AZURE.SUBSCRIPTION.ID}`.

You can discuss this template or leave feedback on our forum https://www.zabbix.com/forum/zabbix-suggestions-and-feedback/

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"
templates.readme:
## Overview

- This template is designed to monitor Microsoft Azure by HTTP.
- It works without any external scripts and uses the script item.
- Currently, the template supports the discovery of virtual machines (VMs), VM scale sets, Cosmos DB for MongoDB, storage accounts, Microsoft SQL, MySQL, and PostgreSQL servers.

## Setup

1. Create an Azure service principal via the Azure command-line interface (Azure CLI) for your subscription.

      `az ad sp create-for-rbac --name zabbix --role reader --scope /subscriptions/<subscription_id>`

> See [Azure documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/create-an-azure-service-principal-azure-cli) for more details.

2. Link the template to a host.
3. Configure the host wizard configuration fields: `Azure App ID`, `Azure password`, `Azure tenant ID`, `Azure subscription ID`.

discovery_rules.name: Storage accounts discovery

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Get data
item_prototypes.description: The HTTP API endpoint that returns storage metrics with the name `[{#NAME}]`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Availability
item_prototypes.description:
The percentage of availability for the storage service or a specified API operation.
Availability is calculated by taking the `TotalBillableRequests` value and dividing it by the number of applicable requests, including those that produced unexpected errors.
All unexpected errors result in reduced availability for the storage service or the specified API operation.

trigger_prototypes.name: Azure: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Availability is low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Azure: Storage account [{#NAME} availability is too low (less than {$AZURE.STORAGE.ACC.AVAILABILITY:"{#NAME}"}%)

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Availability
item_prototypes.description:
The percentage of availability for the storage service or a specified API operation.
Availability is calculated by taking the `TotalBillableRequests` value and dividing it by the number of applicable requests, including those that produced unexpected errors.
All unexpected errors result in reduced availability for the storage service or the specified API operation.

trigger_prototypes.name: Azure: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Availability is low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Azure: Storage account [{#NAME} blob availability are too low (less than {$AZURE.STORAGE.ACC.BLOB.AVAILABILITY:"{#NAME}"}%)

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Capacity
item_prototypes.description: The amount of storage used by the blob service of the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`, expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Container Count
item_prototypes.description: The number of containers in the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Count
item_prototypes.description: The number of blob objects stored in the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Egress
item_prototypes.description:
The amount of egress data. This number includes egress to external client from Azure Storage and also egress within Azure.
As a result, this number does not reflect billable egress.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Index Capacity
item_prototypes.description: The amount of storage with the name `[{#NAME}]` used by the Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 hierarchical index.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Ingress
item_prototypes.description: The amount of ingress data, expressed in bytes. This number includes ingress from an external client into Azure Storage and also ingress within Azure.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Success E2E Latency
item_prototypes.description:
The average end-to-end latency of successful requests made to a storage service or the specified API operation, expressed in milliseconds.
This value includes the required processing time within Azure Storage to read the request, send the response, and receive acknowledgment of the response.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Success Server Latency
item_prototypes.description:
The average time used to process a successful request by Azure Storage.
This value does not include the network latency specified in `SuccessE2ELatency`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Blob Transactions
item_prototypes.description:
The number of requests made to the storage service or a specified API operation.
This number includes successful and failed requests and also requests that produced errors.
Use `ResponseType` dimension for the number of different types of responses.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Egress
item_prototypes.description:
The amount of egress data. This number includes egress to external client from Azure Storage and also egress within Azure.
As a result, this number does not reflect billable egress.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Capacity
item_prototypes.description: The amount of file storage used by the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`, expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Count
item_prototypes.description: The number of files in the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Egress
item_prototypes.description:
The amount of egress data. This number includes egress to external client from Azure Storage and also egress within Azure.
As a result, this number does not reflect billable egress.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Ingress
item_prototypes.description: The amount of ingress data, expressed in bytes. This number includes ingress from an external client into Azure Storage and also ingress within Azure.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Share Capacity Quota
item_prototypes.description: The upper limit on the amount of storage that can be used by Azure Files Service, in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Share Count
item_prototypes.description: The number of file shares in the storage account.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Share Snapshot Size
item_prototypes.description: The amount of storage used by the snapshots in storage account's File service, in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Share Snapshot Count
item_prototypes.description: The number of snapshots present on the share in storage account's Files Service.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: File Success E2E Latency
item_prototypes.description:
The average end-to-end latency of successful requests made to a storage service or the specified API operation, expressed in milliseconds.
This value includes the required processing time within Azure Storage to read the request, send the response, and receive acknowledgment of the response.

item_prototypes.description:
The average time used to process a successful request by Azure Storage.
This value does not include the network latency specified in `SuccessE2ELatency`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Queue Message Count

item_prototypes.description: The amount of storage used by the table service of the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`, expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Table Count
item_prototypes.description: The number of tables in the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`.

item_prototypes.description: The number of table entities in the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Table Ingress
item_prototypes.description: The amount of ingress data, expressed in bytes. This number includes ingress from an external client into Azure Storage and also ingress within Azure.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Table Success E2E Latency
item_prototypes.description:
The average end-to-end latency of successful requests made to a storage service or the specified API operation, expressed in milliseconds.
This value includes the required processing time within Azure Storage to read the request, send the response, and receive acknowledgment of the response.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Table Success Server Latency
item_prototypes.description:
The average time used to process a successful request by Azure Storage.
This value does not include the network latency specified in `SuccessE2ELatency`.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Table Transactions
item_prototypes.description:
The number of requests made to the storage service or a specified API operation.
This number includes successful and failed requests and also requests that produced errors.
Use `ResponseType` dimension for the number of different types of responses.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Transactions
item_prototypes.description:
The number of requests made to the storage service or a specified API operation.
This number includes successful and failed requests and also requests that produced errors.
Use `ResponseType` dimension for the number of different types of responses.

item_prototypes.name: Storage account [{#NAME}]: Used Capacity
item_prototypes.description:
The amount of storage used by the storage account with the name `[{#NAME}]`, expressed in bytes.
For standard storage accounts, it's the sum of capacity used by blob, table, file, and queue.
For premium storage accounts and blob storage accounts, it is the same as BlobCapacity or FileCapacity.
